skill.name: ai-marketing-videos
skill.description: Create AI marketing video concepts and inference.sh CLI workflows for ads, product launches, explainers, and social campaigns.
skill.summary: Build marketing video prompts, ad structures, voiceover steps, and platform formats with inference.sh models.
skill.author: inference-sh

security_audit.summary: Static findings are partly confirmed. The Ruby backtick and weak cryptography alerts are false positives from Markdown text, but the skill does document a curl-to-shell installer, networked CLI model execution, and local file writes. No evidence found of malicious intent, credential theft, prompt injection, or hidden exfiltration beyond the declared inference.sh workflow.

medium_findings.title: Remote Installer Piped To Shell
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The quick start pipes a remote installer directly into sh. This can execute unreviewed code from the network before the user can inspect it. The URL appears related to the declared service, so this is supply-chain risk rather than confirmed malware.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact curl-to-sh pattern is present on line 21. The command is visible in documentation and points to the declared vendor domain, which lowers malicious-intent confidence.

medium_findings.title: Networked CLI Model Execution
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill grants Bash access for infsh commands and shows model runs that send prompts and media instructions to external inference services. This is core functionality, but users may expose campaign details or unreleased product information.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed tool declaration and infsh examples clearly require external service calls. The behavior is declared and relevant to the skill purpose.

medium_findings.title: Shell Workflow Examples Write Local Output Files
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: Several examples run shell workflows and redirect generated outputs into local JSON files. The examples use fixed names and simple variables, so injection risk is limited, but the skill does guide filesystem modification.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file redirections and shell loops are explicit. The examples are documentation patterns, not hidden code paths, so the assessed risk remains moderate.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The reported weak cryptography locations are ordinary Markdown text in the skill description and a Sound Design heading. No cryptographic function, hash algorithm, or credential handling appears at these lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain prose and a heading only. There is no evidence of MD5, DES, SHA1, or any crypto API use.

low_findings.title: Ruby Backtick Execution Labels Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The scanner labels many Markdown fenced Bash examples as Ruby backtick execution. The examples are shell commands, and the actual command and network risks are evaluated separately.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited regions are Markdown code fences containing bash examples. No Ruby source file or Ruby backtick operator is present.

low_findings.title: Placeholder External Audio URL Requires Vetting
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The TikTok workflow uses a hardcoded external audio URL placeholder. This does not show exfiltration, but users should verify media rights, safety, and availability before using external audio.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external URL is explicit and directly used as audio input. It appears illustrative, so the risk is limited.

dangerous_patterns.title: Pipe Remote Script To Shell
dangerous_patterns.description: The command downloads an installer and immediately executes it with sh. Users should inspect installers or use package-manager verification when available.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly present on the cited line. It is dangerous even when the vendor domain is legitimate.

dangerous_patterns.title: External CLI Calls With Generated Marketing Inputs
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill sends marketing prompts and media assembly instructions to an external inference CLI. Sensitive launch plans should be removed before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples repeatedly invoke infsh app run with prompt and media inputs. The data destination is the declared inference.sh service.

content.value_statement: Marketing teams need fast video concepts for many channels and formats. This skill provides prompt patterns, production workflows, and platform-specific guidance for AI-generated ads.
actual_capabilities: Provides templates for product launch, brand story, testimonial, and before-after videos.
Shows inference.sh commands for Veo, Seedance, Kokoro, and media merger workflows.
Structures 15-second, 30-second, and explainer video production flows.
Includes platform guidance for Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, LinkedIn, and Facebook.
Outlines A/B creative variant generation using different ad hooks.
Adds checklist guidance for hooks, captions, product visibility, and call to action.
limitations: Requires access to the inference.sh CLI and supported model providers.
Does not verify ad claims, legal compliance, platform approval, or music rights.
Generated video quality depends on model availability and prompt quality.
Examples may require manual editing before publication or paid campaign use.

use_cases.title: Launch Product Ads
use_cases.description: Create short product reveal concepts, benefit shots, and call-to-action scenes for a new product campaign.

use_cases.title: Create Social Variants
use_cases.description: Generate vertical and square ad ideas for TikTok, Reels, Facebook, and Instagram testing.

use_cases.title: Draft Explainer Videos
use_cases.description: Plan an explainer script, scene list, voiceover, and final assembly workflow for a SaaS offer.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Product Promo
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 15-second marketing video concept for [product]. Include the audience, visual style, opening hook, benefit shot, and final call to action.

prompt_templates.title: Platform Video Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a video ad brief for [platform] using [product]. Include aspect ratio, tone, first three seconds, visual sequence, text overlays, and sound needs.

prompt_templates.title: Explainer Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a 60-second explainer for [offer]. Break it into hook, problem, solution, three features, proof, voiceover direction, and CTA.

prompt_templates.title: Creative Testing Matrix
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate four video ad variants for [campaign]. Vary hook type, emotional angle, product framing, audience segment, and success metric.

output_examples.input: A founder wants a 15-second TikTok ad for a new fitness app.
output: A vertical video concept with a fast problem hook in the first two seconds.
Three quick lifestyle shots showing setup, workout guidance, and progress feedback.
A direct call to action that asks viewers to start a free trial.

output_examples.input: A SaaS team needs a 60-second explainer for a workflow automation tool.
output: A scene-by-scene explainer outline with problem, solution, features, proof, and CTA.
Voiceover guidance with a professional tone and clear benefit statements.
Assembly guidance for combining generated clips and narration.

output_examples.input: A brand manager wants product launch ad variants for paid testing.
output: Four hook options, including problem-focused, product reveal, testimonial, and statistic-led openings.
Platform notes for YouTube pre-roll, Instagram feed, and LinkedIn placements.
A checklist for sound-off viewing, product visibility, brand consistency, and CTA clarity.

best_practices: Remove confidential product details before sending prompts to external inference services.
Write one clear benefit and one call to action for each ad concept.
Adapt aspect ratio, pacing, and captions for each target platform.
anti_patterns: Do not use unverified music URLs or copyrighted audio without permission.
Do not publish generated testimonials that imply real customer statements.
Do not rely on a single generated ad without human review and brand checks.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create prompts and workflows for AI-generated marketing videos, ads, explainers, and product launch assets.

faq.question: Which tools does it use?
faq.answer: It references the inference.sh CLI with video, voiceover, music, and media merger models.

faq.question: Can it make final production-ready ads automatically?
faq.answer: It can guide generation and assembly, but final ads need human review, editing, rights checks, and platform compliance review.

faq.question: Is it safe for confidential launches?
faq.answer: Use caution. The workflows send prompts to external services, so remove confidential names, metrics, and unreleased details.

faq.question: Does it support different ad platforms?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes guidance for TikTok, Reels, Instagram, Facebook, YouTube, LinkedIn, and explainer formats.

faq.question: Why is there a security warning?
faq.answer: The skill documents a remote CLI installer and external model calls. These are legitimate but require user trust and review.
